The Bogleheads 3-fund portfolio combines VTI (US stocks) + VXUS (international stocks) + BND (bonds) for a simple, effective diversified strategy. All fund data and values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.

explanation:
Personal investing and wealth building rely on universal mathematical principles—compound returns, time value of money, and risk-return optimization. This calculator applies industry-standard formulas used by professional investors worldwide.

## How the Bogleheads 3-Fund Strategy Works

The three-fund portfolio divides your investment into:
- **VTI (Vanguard Total Stock Market)**: US stock market exposure
- **VXUS (Vanguard Total International Stock)**: Developed and emerging market stocks
- **BND (Vanguard Total Bond Market)**: US bond market diversification

This approach emphasizes low costs, broad diversification, and minimal ongoing maintenance.

## Historical Performance by Time Horizon

| Time Horizon | US Stocks (S&P 500) | Bonds (10-year) | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4–5% | 3–5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | 2–4% |
| 10 years | ~9–10% average | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Core Investing Principles

- **Stick to the plan**: Emotion is your worst advisor. Stay invested through market downturns.
- **Diversify globally**: Never concentrate in one stock, sector, or country.
- **Minimize costs**: Expense ratios above 1% annually erode ~30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Think long-term**: Equities perform well over 10+ year horizons, not one-year trading windows.
- **Annual rebalancing**: Once per year, no more. This keeps allocations on target and enforces discipline.

## Disclaimers

This calculator provides educational estimates only. For major financial decisions, consult a qualified financial advisor. All data and formulas reflect 2026 standards and are reviewed regularly.

faq.a: A simple, diversified investment strategy using three low-cost index funds: VTI (US stocks), VXUS (international stocks), and BND (bonds). It's designed for buy-and-hold investors who want minimal complexity and low costs.

faq.q: What allocation should I use for VTI, VXUS, and BND?
faq.a: Common allocations vary by age and risk tolerance. A 70/20/10 split (stocks/international/bonds) works for younger investors; older investors might prefer 50/30/20 or more conservative mixes.

faq.q: How often should I rebalance my portfolio?
faq.a: Once per year, typically in January. Rebalancing keeps your allocation aligned with your target and forces you to buy low and sell high.
Results are educational approximations within ±5%. We use internationally validated formulas and 2026 data. For critical financial decisions, consult a professional advisor.

faq.q: Do you store my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send your inputs to any server or store personal information.
Yes. All Hacé Cuentas tools are completely free to use with no registration or login required.

faq.q: What's the difference between VTI and VXUS?
faq.a: VTI tracks the entire US stock market; VXUS tracks international developed and emerging markets. Together, they give you global equity exposure.

faq.q: Where do you get your data?
faq.a: We source from official providers and fund prospectuses. All values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly for accuracy.
